meta.title: Monoclonal Antibodies to Sindbis Virus Glycoprotein E1 can Neutralize, Enhance Infectivity, and Independently Inhibit Haemagglutination or Haemolysis

abstractHtml: Two monoclonal antibodies raised against Sindbis virus were shown to be specific for the envelope glycoprotein E1 by radioimmunoprecipitation (RIP). They had a number of contrasting biological properties. One of them was capable of neutralizing virus infectivity and inhibiting haemagglutination, while the other had no significant neutralizing or haemagglutination-inhibiting capability, but did inhibit virus-mediated haemolysis. Both monoclonal antibodies could enhance virus infectivity of Fc receptor-bearing macrophage-like cells when present at suitable dilutions.
